lesson_title: Advanced Reading and Listening: Speaking Lesson 5
learning_objective: Learners can argue, evaluate, clarify and organise detailed communication in a speaking task about academic topics.
cefr_outcome: Learners can understand main ideas of complex texts and interact with fluency and spontaneity.
learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise speaking for advanced reading and listening. You will learn useful language connected to academic topics and practise advanced passives. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at B2 level.
complete_teaching_input: Focus language: advanced passives. Vocabulary theme: academic topics. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At B2, learners should argue, evaluate, clarify and organise detailed communication with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use advanced passives to talk about academic topics.
Example 2: The learner gives a B2 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for B2.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using advanced passives.
options: Correct use of advanced passives in a B2 sentence.
Incorrect word order with advanced passives.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses advanced passives accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of advanced passives.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the academic topics phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand academic topics.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand academic topics.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form advanced passives. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
homework_task: Write or record five sentences about academic topics using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: B2 Speaking Check - Advanced Reading and Listening
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short speaking task using advanced passives and vocabulary from academic topics.
success_criteria: Uses advanced passives with reasonable accuracy
Uses vocabulary connected to academic topics
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses advanced passives and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to academic topics.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about academic topics. Use at least two examples and one reason.
writing_task: Write a short B2 response about academic topics. Include the target language: advanced passives.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for B2-M03-L05. Speaker one: Today we are practising academic topics. Speaker two: I need to use advanced passives correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: Advanced Passives form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for advanced passives
